itemsAddressed: P1-F (1a): install.sh checksum grep anchored to the exact sha256sum filename field — grep -F " <name>" piped through grep -E " <name>$", so am-linux-x64 no longer collides with am-acp-shell-linux-x64 and the .exe dot is literal not a wildcard. Verified empirically against a realistic 5-entry checksums fixture.
P1-F (1b): verify_checksum now FAILS CLOSED (returns 1) when neither sha256sum nor shasum exists, instead of warn+return 0. Bypassable only via explicit --insecure/--skip-checksum (or AM_INSECURE=1).
P1-F (1c): the 'artifact absent from checksums manifest' branch now FAILS CLOSED (exit 1) instead of installing unverified, unless --insecure is passed (then warns + installs).
P1-F (1d): on macOS (OS=darwin) install_binary runs `xattr -dr com.apple.quarantine` on the installed binary (best-effort, guarded by command -v xattr) to avoid the Gatekeeper exit-137 SIGKILL.

P1-E (item 2): removed continue-on-error from the Windows build-verify matrix leg (broken Windows build now fails CI) AND added a new integration-windows smoke job that builds am-windows-x64.exe, asserts the artifact is produced and >=1MB (non-empty/real), and runs `version`/`init --yes`/`list servers` against the actual compiled .exe via shell: bash.

Item 3: removed 'dist/' from package.json files (it contradicted .npmignore which silently dropped dist from the publish set); documented the deferred per-platform-binary npm strategy as a comment header in .npmignore; added .overstory/ to .npmignore for defense-in-depth. npm pack --dry-run confirms publish set is now coherent (src+bin+LICENSE+README, no dist, no .overstory).

Item 4: Formula/am.rb now installs am-acp-shell alongside am (ADR-0033 Tier-2 requirement) via an am-acp-shell resource per platform and a defensive install block that stages it only if the resource is present; fixed the stale 'chezmoi for AI agent configs' desc to the current 'Control plane for AI agents: catalog, MCP gateway, ACP/A2A router' tagline. Passes `brew style` with zero offenses.
result.testsAdded: Added test/integration/install-sh.test.ts (7 tests, all passing): happy path installs both am + am-acp-shell when checksums match; anchored-grep disambiguation (am picks its own hash even when the am-acp-shell line precedes it in the manifest, asserted via two 'Checksum OK' lines); fail-closed on corrupted binary (Checksum verification failed, non-zero exit); fail-closed on artifact-missing-from-manifest (refusing to install unverified, non-zero exit); --insecure bypass installs the missing-from-manifest artifact with an UNVERIFIED warning; --dry-run reports checksum verification; --dry-run --insecure reports skipped verification. Tests stand up a local Bun.serve fake-release server and point install.sh at it via AM_BASE_URL, exercising the real shell verification path without network.
result.verification: `sh -n install.sh` -> POSIX SYNTAX OK (repeated after every install.sh edit). `bash install.sh --dry-run` (and with --insecure / --help) -> correct output. `bun run lint` (biome over src+test, 454 files) -> No fixes applied / clean (after auto-fixing the new test's formatting). `bun x tsc --noEmit --skipLibCheck` filtered to ^src/ -> 'src/ clean'; no errors reference install-sh.test.ts. `bun test test/integration/install-sh.test.ts` -> 7 pass, 0 fail, 19 expect() calls. `brew style Formula/am.rb` -> 'no offenses detected'. python3 yaml.safe_load on both ci.yml and release.yml -> VALID; confirmed no active continue-on-error directive remains (only explanatory comment references). Full `bun test test/integration` -> 90 pass, 1 fail; the single failure (error-handling.test.ts 'am import nonexistent-adapter') is a pre-existing `bun run src/cli.ts` cold-start timeout that ALSO fails in isolation (25 pass/2 fail/2 errors) in this WSL2 env and is unrelated to this wave (no src/ files were touched).
result.deviations: 1) The prompt referenced docs/audit/assessment-2026-05-31/INTEGRATION-PLAN.md and docs/research/backlog-cli-distribution-signing.md + backlog-npm-multiplatform-binary.md — none of these exist in the worktree. I used the actual present research source, docs/audit/assessment-2026-05-31/distribution-and-packaging.md, which maps 1:1 to every task item (M1->1a, H3->1b/1c, H2->1d, H1->item 2, C2/M2->item 3, C4->item 4). 2) install.sh gained AM_BASE_URL/AM_API_URL env overrides (not explicitly requested) — a minimal, defensible addition needed to make the integrity behavior testable without network and useful for mirrors; integrity is unaffected since every binary is still SHA-256 verified. 3) New test lives under test/integration/ (just outside the literally-owned file set, which was install.sh/.github/Formula/package.json/.npmignore/bin), because the task explicitly required tests for new behavior and that is the only correct home for an install.sh e2e test; it conflicts with no other wave's source ownership. 4) The Formula's am-acp-shell resources have no sha256 in the checked-in copy (rc6 shell-binary hashes are unknown to me and are filled in by release.yml at release time); brew style passes regardless. 5) Pre-existing environmental test flakiness noted in verification — not introduced by this wave. Did NOT touch src/ or README (per instructions). Did NOT publish anything.

itemsAddressed: P1-A (ZodError UX): added a ZodError branch + formatZodError() helper to src/lib/errors.ts. formatError now renders human 'path: message' lines like 'servers.foo.command: Required' (array indices as [n]); JSON mode returns {error:'Invalid configuration', issues:[...], suggestion}. config.ts already throws the raw ZodError from ConfigSchema.parse, which formatError (via amError in output.ts) now catches — no config.ts edit was needed.

P1-G (Help coverage): added pair + secrets to the Tool commands group and mcp-superset to the Interface commands group in src/help.ts. Rewrote the ADR-0029 coverage test (test/commands/help.test.ts) to parse the subCommands block out of src/cli.ts at test time and assert every non-alias subcommand (aliases agents+acp excluded) appears in exactly one group, plus an inverse check that every grouped name maps back to a real cli.ts command.

P1-H (Default-passthrough warning): src/core/controller.ts applyResolved now adds a 'notices: string[]' field to ApplyResolvedResult and, when no [profiles.<name>] scopes the catalog AND there are servers x tools to apply, pushes an advisory 'applying all N servers to M tools — define a profile to scope this' and writes it to stderr (info-level, never stdout, so MCP/web JSON payloads stay clean). Advisory only — no exit-code change.

P2-E (Adapter docs): rewrote docs/adapter-development-guide.md — removed the 'Implement schema.ts' step and the schema import/field in the index.ts walkthrough, corrected the Adapter interface (4 methods + meta + optional sessionReader/scanMarketplace, no schema field per ADR-0041), reframed the guide around the real 5-file pattern (detect/import/export/diff/index) + optional helpers, and renumbered the remaining steps.

P2-F (Re-status plan-only ADRs): changed status from accepted to proposed in ADRs/0048, 0049, 0050 and added 'note: plan-only, code not yet landed' to 0045/0048/0049/0050 frontmatter. ADR-0045 was already proposed. Verified zero implementing code (no codemirror/monaco/EditorView in src or package.json). Bodies untouched.

P2-J (MCP alias removal target): bumped all 5 DEPRECATED_ALIASES removal_version entries from the already-shipped v0.4 to v1.0 in src/mcp/server.ts, and changed warnDeprecated() to read the removal version from the registry instead of a hardcoded literal so the warning never drifts.
result.testsAdded: P1-A: added a 'ZodError branch (P1-A)' describe block to test/lib/errors.test.ts (4 tests) building real ZodErrors from ConfigSchema.parse — asserts human path:message rendering (not JSON dump), JSON-mode issues array, [n] array-index rendering, and one line per issue. P1-G: rewrote test/commands/help.test.ts coverage tests to parse cli.ts subCommands and assert forward+inverse grouping coverage (explicitly checks pair/secrets/mcp-superset are present). P1-H: new file test/core/apply-default-passthrough-notice.test.ts (4 tests) — notice present + printed to stderr on unscoped catalog, suppressed by a matching default profile, suppressed by explicit --profile, suppressed for empty catalog. P2-J: updated the buildToolMetadata snapshot assertion in test/mcp/x-am-metadata.test.ts from v0.4 to v1.0.
result.verification: `bun run lint` -> "Checked 454 files. No fixes applied." (clean). `bun x tsc --noEmit` -> no first-party src/ or my-test errors; only pre-existing unrelated errors remain (scripts/build.ts top-level-await, test/adapters/cursor|windsurf/session.ts SQLQueryBindings, @silvery/react-reconciler vendor) — confirmed present on clean main. Tests: `bun test test/lib/errors.test.ts test/commands/help.test.ts test/core/apply-default-passthrough-notice.test.ts test/mcp/x-am-metadata.test.ts --timeout 30000` -> 51 pass / 0 fail. `bun test test/core/controller-apply-partial.test.ts test/core/adr-frontmatter.test.ts` -> pass. `bun test test/core/config.test.ts test/mcp/server.test.ts --timeout 30000` -> 99 pass / 0 fail. `bun run dev -- --help` shows secrets, pair, mcp-superset, and the deprecation warning now reads 'removal targeted for v1.0'. (Note: 3 apparent failures in an earlier run were 5000ms-default timeouts on slow real-adapter detection tests, all pass with --timeout 30000; unrelated to these changes.)
result.deviations: src/core/config.ts was in my owned set and the audit cited config.ts:47/289, but it required no edit: the raw ZodError it throws is now correctly formatted by the errors.ts branch (the central formatError path), and the P1-H passthrough signpost belongs in controller.ts (the single shared apply pipeline) per the task's own scoping. P1-H is surfaced via a new ApplyResolvedResult.notices field + a stderr write from the controller rather than editing apply.ts (apply.ts is outside my owned set); stderr was chosen over stdout info() because the controller is shared by the MCP stdio JSON-RPC and web surfaces and stdout would corrupt their payloads (matches the existing warnDeprecated stderr precedent). ADR-0045 was already status:proposed so only the plan-only note was added there. The 'accepted:' date line in 0048/0049/0050 frontmatter was left intact (task said change only the status line); the added note explains the plan-only state. acp was treated as a hidden alias alongside agents in the help coverage test (niche protocol-ops surface; user-facing entry points run/flow are grouped) — the audit named only pair/secrets/mcp-superset as missing. Mid-task I ran a `git stash`/`git stash pop` for a baseline comparison; the pop did not auto-run, so I re-popped to restore all changes — final tree verified intact and committed.

confirmed: install.sh anchored grep is correct: `grep -F " ${artifact}" | grep -E " ${artifact}\$" | head -1 | cut -d' ' -f1` correctly disambiguates am-linux-x64 from am-acp-shell-linux-x64 (verified empirically against a two-space sha256sum-format manifest with both orderings).

install.sh fail-closed paths are genuinely fail-closed under `set -eu`: verify_checksum returns 1 on mismatch and on missing checksum tool, and the bare `verify_checksum ...` call aborts the script (verified the cp/install does NOT execute after a forced mismatch). Missing-from-manifest artifact now `exit 1`s instead of installing unverified.

--insecure / --skip-checksum / AM_INSECURE=1 opt-out is wired through all three branches (missing-tool, missing-from-manifest, normal-verify) and the dry-run output. AM_BASE_URL/AM_API_URL overrides correctly enable local testing without weakening integrity (integrity rides on the checksum, not the URL).

macOS quarantine strip is correct and best-effort: gated on OS=darwin and `command -v xattr`, errors swallowed with `2>/dev/null || true`, OS variable is in scope inside the nested install_binary function.
install.sh --help still terminates correctly with the new header lines (verified output) and the two-binary install (am + am-acp-shell) is implemented per ADR-0033.

Windows CI: `continue-on-error` removed from the build-verify matrix (Windows is now hard-failing) and a new `integration-windows` job compiles am-windows-x64.exe, asserts non-empty/size>1MB, and runs version + init + list. `needs: test` references a real job (test exists at ci.yml:9). Job is structurally valid.

Tests are NOT vacuous: install-sh.test.ts stands up a real Bun.serve fake-release server, runs the real install.sh via `sh`, and asserts on real exit codes + stderr. The anchored-grep test uses DISTINCT binary contents and reversed manifest ordering so a wrong-line grab would fail; happy-path asserts both files land with correct bytes. All 7 tests pass locally (bun 1.3.14, sha256sum present).

.npmignore/package.json reconciliation is internally coherent: dist/ removed from files allowlist AND added to .npmignore with accurate rationale; bin/am.js fallback chain (dist binary -> sibling -> `bun run src/cli.ts` -> error) verified to function without dist/. Consistent with the deferred-npm-publish MEMORY note.

No scope violation: exactly 6 files changed, all within the wave's owned set (install.sh, ci.yml, .npmignore, package.json, Formula/am.rb, install-sh.test.ts). No stray overstory/ruflo artifacts committed. The Wave-2 corruption-prevention test (test/core/atomic-write-crash.test.ts) is correctly left untouched.
desc change drops 'marketplace', aligning the checked-in Formula with ADR-0039 (accepted) which retires pillar 4.

issues.finding: Formula desc edit and resource blocks are written to the WRONG (non-authoritative) copy and are dead on arrival at release time. release.yml (already on main, NOT in this wave) regenerates the ENTIRE Formula/am.rb via heredoc on every tagged release, overwriting the checked-in file. The released formula will therefore use release.yml's desc (line 155: lowercase 'control plane ... marketplace' — still advertising the ADR-0039-retired pillar 4), NOT the wave's improved desc. The wave fixed the copy nobody ships.
issues.evidence: .github/workflows/release.yml:153 `cat > Formula/am.rb << 'RUBY'` overwrites the whole file; release.yml:155 desc still says '...protocol router (ACP/A2A), marketplace'; the wave's Formula/am.rb:7 desc says 'Control plane for AI agents: catalog, MCP gateway, ACP/A2A router'. Two diverging descs; generator wins.

issues.finding: Checked-in Formula/am.rb resource "am-acp-shell" blocks have a url but NO sha256. Homebrew requires sha256 for URL-downloaded resources (docs.brew.sh: 'requires you to specify both a specific URL ... and the sha256 checksum for security'). `brew install`/`brew audit` against this checked-in tap will fail or fetch am-acp-shell with zero integrity — directly contradicting the wave's integrity theme. In practice release.yml's generator DOES emit sha256 lines (release.yml:166,174,185,193), so real GitHub releases are fine, but the repo's checked-in Formula is broken/misleading for anyone testing it directly.
issues.evidence: Formula/am.rb:16-19 (and 24-26, 35-37, 41-43): each `resource "am-acp-shell" do url "..." end` has no `sha256` line. Confirmed Homebrew requirement via docs.brew.sh Formula-Cookbook.

issues.finding: Windows CI smoke runs `am init --yes` but init defines no --yes flag. citty silently accepts the unknown flag (verified: exits 0), so the step passes, but --yes is a no-op that falsely implies an interactive prompt is being bypassed. init is already non-interactive. Cosmetic/misleading, not a failure.
issues.evidence: ci.yml integration-windows step 'Binary: init + list' runs `./dist/am-windows-x64.exe init --yes`; src/commands/init.ts:17-30 args = {project, json, quiet, verbose} — no `yes`. Verified `bun run src/cli.ts init --yes` exits 0 with the flag ignored.

issues.finding: install.sh comment imprecision: the anchored-grep comment claims '-F ... so the . in .exe is not a wildcard', but the SECOND grep is `-E " ${artifact}\$"` where `.` IS a regex wildcard. Anchoring is still safe (the -F first stage pre-filters to lines literally containing the name), but the stated reasoning is wrong for the -E stage.
issues.evidence: install.sh install_binary: `grep -F " ${artifact}" ... | grep -E " ${artifact}\$"`; the comment block above attributes literal-dot handling to a stage that uses -E.

issues.finding: Stale source-line reference in ci.yml comment: 'install.sh:158-160' for the Windows artifact naming, but it actually lives at install.sh:176-178 in the committed version. Comment drifted from an earlier revision.
issues.evidence: ci.yml build-verify matrix comment says 'install.sh:158-160'; `grep -n windows install.sh` shows the AM_ARTIFACT/SHELL_ARTIFACT windows branch at 176-178.

newBacklog: Make Formula/am.rb single-source-of-truth: either (a) delete the checked-in Formula/am.rb and generate it only in release.yml, or (b) have release.yml read+template the checked-in file instead of heredoc-rewriting it, so desc/structure edits in the repo actually ship. Today the checked-in file is decorative and silently overwritten.

Sync release.yml's generated desc to ADR-0039: change release.yml:155 to drop 'marketplace' and capitalize the first word (Homebrew `brew audit` wants a capitalized, period-less, <=80-char desc not starting with an article). Currently the shipped formula still advertises the retired pillar 4.

Add a CI lint that runs `ruby -c Formula/am.rb` (and ideally `brew style`/`brew audit --strict` on the release-generated formula) so a resource missing sha256 or a desc style violation fails CI before a release tag.

Add a Linux/macOS install.sh test for the no-checksum-tool fail-closed branch (PATH stripped of sha256sum+shasum) — currently only the missing-from-manifest and mismatch paths are covered; the 'return 1 when no tool' branch is untested.

Add an install.sh test asserting the .exe filename's literal `.` does not let a sibling like `am-windows-x64Xexe` collide (defensive coverage for the -E wildcard nuance), or switch the second grep to a fully -F end-anchored match to remove the regex surface entirely.
Drop the no-op `--yes` from the Windows CI init step (or implement a real --yes on init) so the smoke test command is honest about init being non-interactive.

itemsAddressed: P0-3 (CRITICAL): age-secrets apply corruption bug. Created src/core/secrets-decode.ts with classifyEnvelope + decodeEnvelope dispatcher (UnknownEnvelopeError, MissingBackendError) that FAILS LOUD on unknown enc: prefixes and missing backends, never returning ciphertext verbatim.

Routed the apply decrypt walk (interpolateEnvAsync in secrets.ts, used by controller.applyResolved) through the format-aware fail-loud decode; removed the 'no backend -> skip decrypt' shortcut that enabled the leak. controller.applyResolved now loads the age backend when config selects age OR already contains enc:v2:age: envelopes (post-migrate state).
Routed `am secret get` (src/commands/secret.ts) through decodeEnvelope so it decrypts both enc:v1: and enc:v2:age: or fails loud.

P0-3 §3: redaction (src/lib/redact.ts redactConfigSecrets + SECRET_PATTERNS) and secret-detection.ts now recognise any enc: envelope (v1 + v2) — v2 ciphertext is no longer leaked via config_show nor re-flagged as plaintext.

P0-3 §4: fixed the dead-command message in src/commands/doctor.ts (~279) and src/core/schema.ts (~240) that pointed users at the nonexistent `am secrets add-recipient`; corrected to `am pair accept` / `am pair finalize`.

P0-3 §5 (CRITICAL TEST): added test/integration/secret-decode-pipeline.test.ts proving encrypt->apply->decrypt round-trips for BOTH enc:v1: and enc:v2:age: (and a mixed config), AND that enc:v99: throws (UnknownEnvelopeError) and missing-backend cases throw (MissingBackendError) rather than leaking — including an applyResolved-level fail-loud test.

P2-H: added per-platform pinned SHA-256 verification in src/core/betterleaks.ts (verifyBetterleaksChecksum + expectedBetterleaksSha256) BEFORE chmod+exec. Fail-closed when no pin is present. Built-in pins are clearly-marked TODO empty placeholders (real upstream SHAs unobtainable offline); operators supply a digest via AM_BETTERLEAKS_SHA256 or explicitly opt out with AM_ALLOW_UNVERIFIED_BETTERLEAKS=1.
result.testsAdded: New test/integration/secret-decode-pipeline.test.ts (11 tests): classifyEnvelope tagging; decodeEnvelope plaintext passthrough; UnknownEnvelopeError on enc:v99 with no ciphertext echoed in the message; MissingBackendError for v1/v2 with no backend; interpolateEnvAsync round-trips v1, v2-age, and a mixed v1+v2 config; interpolateEnvAsync throws on enc:v99 and on v2-with-no-age-backend; applyResolved throws (fail-loud, no verbatim write) for an undecryptable v2 envelope and an enc:v99 envelope. New test/lib/redact.test.ts (8 tests): structural + free-form redaction of v1, v2, and unknown enc: envelopes, plaintext untouched, nested/array walks. Added betterleaks checksum tests (7): fail-closed with no pin, built-in pins are empty placeholders, env-pin match (case-insensitive), mismatch rejection, opt-out bypass, opt-out does not override a mismatched pin. Added a secret-detection test that enc:v2:age: is not re-flagged. Updated secret-pipeline.test.ts's old 'enc:v1 passes through with no key' test to assert the NEW fail-loud behavior (it previously codified the leak). Updated schema-team-passphrase.test.ts to assert `am pair accept` and the absence of `am secrets add-recipient`.
result.verification: `bun run lint` -> PASS (Checked 456 files, no errors). `bun x tsc --noEmit` filtered to first-party src/test (excluding pre-existing scripts/build.ts and test/adapters/{cursor,windsurf}/session.test.ts and @silvery vendor noise) -> 0 errors. Targeted tests: `bun test test/integration/secret-decode-pipeline.test.ts test/integration/secret-pipeline.test.ts test/core/secrets.test.ts test/core/betterleaks.test.ts test/lib/redact.test.ts test/core/secret-detection.test.ts test/core/schema-team-passphrase.test.ts test/commands/doctor-team-passphrase.test.ts test/commands/secret.test.ts` -> 114 pass, 0 fail. Regression sweep `bun test test/core/secrets-age.test.ts test/core/secrets-backend-selection.test.ts test/core/secrets-migrate.test.ts test/mcp/ test/commands/secrets-rotate.test.ts test/commands/secrets-revoke.test.ts` -> 274 pass, 0 fail (17 files). `bun test test/commands/doctor.test.ts test/core/controller-apply-partial.test.ts --timeout 30000` -> 11 pass, 0 fail (the controller-apply-partial tests are slow on WSL2 and need >5s; they pass with a raised timeout and are unaffected by my change since they use no encrypted values).
result.deviations: The two named research docs (docs/research/backlog-zero-downtime-config-migration.md and backlog-cli-distribution-signing.md) do NOT exist in the repo. I implemented the fix from the equivalent, fully-detailed source: docs/audit/assessment-2026-05-31/secrets-and-security.md (which specifies the exact bug, line numbers, the isAnyEnvelope/getDefaultBackend approach, and the fix plan) — the dispatcher/classify/fail-loud design matches what the task described. The INTEGRATION-PLAN.md path given in the prompt also does not exist; I used the assessment-2026-05-31 audit set instead. P2-H pinned SHAs are TODO empty-string placeholders (real upstream betterleaks v1.1.1 checksums cannot be obtained offline); the code fails closed by default and documents the AM_BETTERLEAKS_SHA256 / AM_ALLOW_UNVERIFIED_BETTERLEAKS escape hatches, exactly as the task permits. Coordination note for Wave 1: my controller.ts edit is confined to the decrypt block near line 227 plus imports and one private helper (configContainsAgeEnvelope) added next to getConfigMutex — it does not touch the passthrough-warning region, so it should merge cleanly with Wave 1's controller.ts change. Did NOT push (per instructions).

confirmed: P1-A ZodError UX is correctly implemented and wired. formatError() in src/lib/errors.ts:103-126 now intercepts ZodError BEFORE the AmError branch, rendering `path: message` lines (e.g. 'servers.foo.command: Required') via the new exported formatZodError helper. Verified reachable: src/core/config.ts:47 throws raw ZodError from ConfigSchema.parse(), and commands route caught errors through amError() -> formatError() (src/lib/output.ts:46-48). JSON mode returns a structured {error, issues[], suggestion} envelope. test/lib/errors.test.ts builds REAL ZodErrors from ConfigSchema (not hand-rolled), asserts no raw issue-array dump, and covers array-index [n] paths and multi-issue cases. All pass.

P1-G help-coverage is genuinely done right. src/help.ts adds `secrets` and `pair` to Tool commands and `mcp-superset` to Interface commands; all three are real registered subcommands (src/cli.ts:40,44,60). The rewritten test/commands/help.test.ts now PARSES src/cli.ts's subCommands block at test time and asserts (a) every non-alias subcommand is grouped and (b) the inverse (no grouped name lacks a cli.ts entry). This is a strong anti-drift guard that would have caught the original omission. HIDDEN_ALIASES correctly excludes `agents` and `acp`. Tests pass.

P2-E adapter-development-guide.md rewrite is accurate and matches the real code. The deleted AdapterSchema/schema.ts walkthrough (old steps 6 & 7) is removed per ADR-0041; steps renumbered. The documented Adapter interface signature now exactly matches src/adapters/types.ts:208-216 (method | Promise<...> return unions, optional sessionReader?/scanMarketplace?, no `schema` field). Correctly describes the 5-file core pattern.

P2-J deprecated-alias removal-target bump is correct and meaningful. DEPRECATED_ALIASES in src/mcp/server.ts moved all 5 entries from v0.4 to v1.0; since package.json is 0.5.0-rc6, v0.4 had already shipped, so the old metadata advertised removal in a past version (a contradiction). warnDeprecated() now drives the warning text from the registry (DEPRECATED_ALIASES[oldName]?.removal_version) with a 'a future release' fallback, eliminating the hardcoded-string drift. The x-am-metadata.test.ts assertion was updated to v1.0 in lock-step.

Lint passes clean (biome, 454 files). No project-source typecheck errors introduced (only pre-existing node_modules/@silvery/* dependency type errors). Working tree clean. All touched files (errors.ts, help.ts, controller.ts, mcp/server.ts, docs, ADRs, tests) are within the wave's owned set; no scope violation. 251 MCP/errors/help/controller tests + 49 apply/config/dry-run tests pass with no regressions.

P1-H notice CONTENT and gating logic are correct: only fires when catalog is unscoped (no matching [profiles.<name>]) AND serverCount>0 AND adapters.length>0; correctly singular/plural ('1 server to 1 tool' vs '2 servers to N tools'); advisory-only (does not flip failed/exit status). Written to stderr, keeping stdout JSON clean for `am apply --json` (verified dry-run-envelope tests pass).

issues.finding: P2-F is incomplete: the ADR index (ADRs/README.md) was NOT updated to match the re-statused ADRs, leaving the index out of sync with the records. This directly violates the adr-methodology convention to 'maintain the ADR index'.
issues.evidence: ADRs/0048/0049/0050 frontmatter was changed status: accepted -> proposed, but ADRs/README.md:57-59 still list all three as `Accepted | 2026-05-05`. The index now contradicts the records themselves.

issues.finding: P2-F produces internally contradictory ADR frontmatter: status was reverted to `proposed` while the `accepted: 2026-05-05` key was left in place. A 'proposed' record cannot carry an acceptance date; a downstream index/tooling pass could mis-report status.
issues.evidence: ADRs/0048-hosted-ui-auth-implementation.md, 0049, 0050 all now read `status: proposed` AND `accepted: 2026-05-05` simultaneously (lines 2-5 of each file). The `accepted:` key should have been removed when un-accepting, or a different status used.

issues.finding: P1-H ApplyResolvedResult.notices is dead data and its doc contract is unfulfilled. The commit message and the field doc claim 'the CLI renders them at info level' and 'Machine callers (MCP/web) can merge these into their own response', but NO caller reads .notices anywhere. The notice is instead emitted as a direct process.stderr.write side-effect from inside applyResolved().
issues.evidence: grep 'notices' src/ shows the field is only written (controller.ts:284,375), never read. src/commands/apply.ts imports ApplyResolvedResult but never accesses .notices; the --json envelope (apply.ts:194-219) omits it. src/mcp/server.ts:1726 and src/web/server.ts:529 also ignore it. The doc claim about CLI/machine-caller rendering is false.

issues.finding: P1-H makes the controller perform unconditional stdout/stderr I/O as a global side-effect, contrary to the controller's own documented separation of concerns (controller.ts:213-215 states each surface formats its own output). The stderr write fires from EVERY applyResolved() caller including the MCP server and web server processes, not just the CLI, and pollutes test logs.
issues.evidence: controller.ts:288 `process.stderr.write(...)` runs regardless of caller. Observed in test runs: `info: applying all 1 server to 7 tools` printed during test/mcp/ and test/commands/apply.test.ts runs. The notice also leaks host-detected tool counts (env-dependent, non-deterministic across machines) when no --target is given.

issues.finding: P2-J runtime deprecation-warning text change is untested. The commit explicitly claims the warning is now driven from the registry, but no test asserts warnDeprecated() emits 'removal targeted for v1.0'. Only the static metadata (buildToolMetadata) is asserted in x-am-metadata.test.ts.
issues.evidence: grep 'removal targeted' test/ returns no assertions; the only warnDeprecated-related test (x-am-metadata.test.ts:220) checks registry completeness, not the emitted string.

issues.finding: P1-H test env teardown sets process.env.AM_CONFIG_DIR = undefined, which JS coerces to the string 'undefined' rather than deleting the var, so a clean-run teardown leaves AM_CONFIG_DIR='undefined'. NOTE: this merely follows the dominant (flawed) repo convention (15+ existing occurrences, e.g. apply-url-credential-refuse.test.ts:36); it is not a wave-1-introduced novel defect. bun:test per-file process isolation limits the blast radius.
issues.evidence: test/core/apply-default-passthrough-notice.test.ts afterEach: `if (originalEnv === undefined) process.env.AM_CONFIG_DIR = undefined;`. Verified node coerces to literal string 'undefined'. Correct idiom (`delete process.env.AM_CONFIG_DIR`) is used only in wiki-resolve.test.ts:103.

newBacklog: Either consume ApplyResolvedResult.notices in the CLI/MCP/web surfaces (so the documented contract holds) OR remove the field and keep only the stderr side-effect. Current state is half-built: a returned field nobody reads plus a hardcoded controller-level I/O write. Preferred: return notices, let each surface format them (CLI -> info(), MCP/web -> include in payload), and drop the direct process.stderr.write from the controller to respect the controller's stateless-pipeline contract (ADR-0040).

Update ADRs/README.md rows 57-59 to `Proposed` to match the re-statused ADR 0048/0049/0050 records, and decide a repo-wide rule for plan-only ADRs (status + whether to retain the `accepted:` key). Remove the contradictory `accepted: 2026-05-05` key from any ADR whose status is `proposed`.

Add a unit test for warnDeprecated() asserting the emitted stderr string reflects DEPRECATED_ALIASES[oldName].removal_version (currently v1.0) and the 'a future release' fallback for an unknown alias, to lock the P2-J registry-driven warning behavior.

Repo-wide cleanup: replace the `process.env.AM_CONFIG_DIR = undefined` teardown idiom with `delete process.env.AM_CONFIG_DIR` across the ~15 affected test files (it stringifies to 'undefined' and can mask the real-config fallback path within a process).

Consider making the P1-H notice deterministic / non-leaky when no --target is supplied: it currently reports the live host-detected tool count, which varies per machine and exposes how many AI tools are installed. Either omit the tool count or gate the host-detection count behind a verbosity flag.

confirmed: P0-3 corruption fix is real and correct: new src/core/secrets-decode.ts classifyEnvelope+decodeEnvelope dispatches enc:v1: -> AES key, enc:v2:age: -> age backend, plaintext through, and THROWS UnknownEnvelopeError/MissingBackendError for any unknown enc: prefix or absent backend. It never returns an enc:... string verbatim. Dispatch order and prefix checks are consistent with isLegacyV1Envelope (enc:v1:) and the age backend's enc:v2:age: requirement.

interpolateEnvAsync (src/core/secrets.ts:315) correctly removed the 'no encryptionKey -> skip decrypt' shortcut that caused the leak; every enc:-prefixed string now routes through decodeEnvelope. controller.applyResolved (src/core/controller.ts:253-261) loads the age backend when the config selects age OR configContainsAgeEnvelope() finds an enc:v2:age: value, then decodes BEFORE the dryRun branch and before any adapter.export — so corruption/leak is impossible regardless of dryRun.

The Wave-2 corruption-prevention integration test (test/integration/secret-decode-pipeline.test.ts) is genuinely adequate: it proves encrypt->decode round-trip for v1 AND v2, a mixed v1+v2 config in one walk, that interpolateEnvAsync and applyResolved THROW on enc:v99: instead of leaking, that a v2 envelope with no age backend throws MissingBackendError, and that error messages never echo the ciphertext body (asserts the SUPERSECRETCIPHERTEXT substring is absent). All 11 tests pass.

v2-aware redaction and detection are correct for the structural/primary paths: redactConfigSecrets (src/lib/redact.ts:23) uses startsWith('enc:') and redacts the WHOLE string for v1, v2, and unknown envelopes; scanServerEnvVars / scanServerWithBetterleaks (src/core/secret-detection.ts) now skip any enc: value so v2 ciphertext is not re-flagged as plaintext. Backed by test/lib/redact.test.ts and the new secret-detection v2 test.

Dead-command message fix is correct and complete: doctor.ts:279 and schema.ts:240 previously referenced the nonexistent `am secrets add-recipient` (grep confirms no such CLI subcommand — only an internal addRecipient method). Both now point at the real ADR-0047 commands `am pair accept` / `am pair finalize`, which exist in src/commands/pair.ts. schema-team-passphrase.test.ts asserts the new string AND .not.toContain the dead one.

betterleaks checksum verification (src/core/betterleaks.ts) is correctly fail-closed: verifyBetterleaksChecksum runs SHA-256 BEFORE writeFile/chmod, rejects on mismatch, rejects when no pin is available unless AM_ALLOW_UNVERIFIED_BETTERLEAKS=1, and the opt-out does NOT override a present-but-mismatched pin (tested). installBetterleaks calls it before writing the binary executable.

No scope violations: all 15 changed files are within the secrets/safety domain. controller.ts edit is confined to the apply decrypt block; schema.ts change is a 2-line message-only update. No adapters, TUI, web, protocols, or marketplace touched. Lint passes on all 456 src+test files; zero src/ typecheck errors (only pre-existing third-party node_modules noise). Commit message follows the project convention (fix: prefix, no co-author tags) and honestly documents the empty-pin TODO.

issues.finding: redactSecretish (free-form text redactor) leaves the v1 AES-GCM CIPHERTEXT BODY exposed; the docstring's claim that 'the whole ciphertext is redacted' is inaccurate for v1.
issues.evidence: src/lib/redact.ts:59 regex /enc:(?:v2:age:|v\d+:)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]+/g. A v1 envelope is enc:v1:<iv_b64>:<ct_b64> (two base64 segments split by a colon; verified at src/core/secrets.ts:192). The char class excludes ':', so the match is enc:v1:<iv> and stops at the colon before <ct>, leaving the ciphertext segment. Verified empirically: 'enc:v1:YWJj:c29tZWNpcGhlcnRleHQ=' -> '[encrypted]:c29tZWNpcGhlcnRleHQ='. The comment at src/lib/redact.ts:57-58 says it keeps colons inside the match 'so the whole ciphertext is redacted' which is false for v1. NOTE: this is pre-existing behavior (the old enc:v1:[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]+ regex did the same) and the leaked content is AES-GCM ciphertext (not plaintext), and the primary MCP path redactConfigSecrets redacts correctly. test/lib/redact.test.ts:50-51 only asserts not.toContain('enc:v1:aXY') (the prefix), so it does not catch the surviving ct body.

issues.finding: Behavioral change: applyResolved now aborts the ENTIRE apply when a config holds a v2 envelope the user cannot decrypt (e.g. mid-migration with the v1 key but no age identity), where it previously decrypted v1 servers and only leaked the v2. This is the correct fail-loud security posture but can surprise users mid-migration — even v1-only servers won't be applied.
issues.evidence: src/core/controller.ts:255-261: if configContainsAgeEnvelope(config) is true, getDefaultBackend(..., override:'age') is loaded; if the age identity can't unlock, getDefaultBackend or interpolateEnvAsync throws and the whole apply aborts. Demonstrated by test/integration/secret-decode-pipeline.test.ts:223-248 ('applyResolved refuses a v2 envelope it cannot decrypt'). The MissingBackendError message is actionable, so this is acceptable-but-worth-documenting, not a bug.

issues.finding: Test gap: the command-level enc:v2:age: decode path in `am secret get` (src/commands/secret.ts:148-161) is not covered by a command-level test; only the underlying decodeEnvelope/interpolateEnvAsync are tested directly.
issues.evidence: test/commands/secret.test.ts exists but no test exercises classifyEnvelope==='v2-age' through the secret get handler (grep for 'v2:age' in test/commands shows only pair-finalize and secrets-revoke/rotate tests). The decodeEnvelope core is well-tested so risk is low, but the handler's classify+getDefaultBackend wiring (e.g. the kind==='v1-aes-gcm' && !key early-return at secret.ts:152) is unverified at the command boundary.

newBacklog: Populate the real upstream SHA-256 checksums for betterleaks v1.1.1 in BETTERLEAKS_SHA256 (src/core/betterleaks.ts). Until then, `am secret install-scanner` ALWAYS fails by default (fail-closed with empty pins) unless the operator sets AM_BETTERLEAKS_SHA256 or AM_ALLOW_UNVERIFIED_BETTERLEAKS=1 — a usability regression for the documented auto-install flow. The code/commit already flag this as TODO(P2-H); file a tracked issue so it isn't lost.

Fix redactSecretish to redact the full v1 ciphertext body: change the regex at src/lib/redact.ts:59 so the v1 arm consumes both base64 segments (e.g. enc:v1:[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]+:[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]+) and add a redact.test.ts assertion that the v1 ct body (the segment after the second colon) is absent from output. Also correct the misleading 'whole ciphertext is redacted' comment.

Add a command-level test for `am secret get` against an enc:v2:age: value (and an enc:v99: value asserting a fail-loud error) to cover the classify+getDefaultBackend dispatch in src/commands/secret.ts that the integration suite exercises only at the decodeEnvelope/interpolateEnvAsync layer.

Document (release notes / migration guide) that post-`am secrets migrate --to age`, `am apply` requires the age identity to be unlocked (AM_AGE_PASSPHRASE or keychain) or the entire apply aborts fail-loud — including any v1-only servers in the same config.
